response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack breadth, distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic (low-latency, high-throughput services), and genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role demands modern C++ or Rust as the primary stack, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with no Rust. Core experience is in DLang, TypeScript, and Node.js, not the production C++ routing-pipeline work this role requires. Worth a screening call to probe C++ depth and willingness to ramp, but not a strong on-paper match.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O, fibers)
Senior IC track record across embedded, backend, and infrastructure
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience in the last 10 years; only legacy C/C++ at NI in 2014-2015
No navigation/routing pipeline or map-tiling background
Recent work is TypeScript/Node-heavy, far from the target stack
